libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker service or environment/system property appears required. Prefer in-memory XML, WebDAV method/property objects, and lightweight servlet request/response mocks or stubs rather than starting a servlet container or external WebDAV server.

deterministic_setup.reason:
The artifact declares javax.servlet:servlet-api as provided and source classes such as AbstractWebdavServlet, DavServletRequest, DavServletResponse, WebdavRequestImpl, InputContextImpl, and OutputContextImpl import javax.servlet APIs; existing coverage for this library also adds servlet-api explicit
...[truncated]

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8851 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.apache.jackrabbit:jackrabbit-webdav:2.0-beta4; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: A generated scaffold without the explicit servlet API dependency may only cover client/property classes and fail to compile or avoid meaningful server/request coverage.
SLF4J has no required binding for coverage; missing bindings should only produce logging warnings unless tests assert logging behavior.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Jackrabbit WebDAV is an in-process WebDAV client/server utility library, but its public server/request/context APIs depend on the Servlet API declared as Maven provided, so meaningful tests need servlet classes added explicitly to the test classpath.
